title: Apexguard Auto Film: vectorize the AI-drafted master + two sub-brand marks into production SVGs and assemble a multi-size die-cut vinyl decal sheet on the supplied cut-line template
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Upwork brief: the client "supplies paint protection film (PPF), window tint, and related products", is "rebranding and need a designer to deliver a complete logo and identity system that works on digital channels and physical packaging (boxes, labels, stickers)"; visual direction = "Premium, modern, performance-automotive. Minimalist and confident. Not flashy, not factory-catalog, not 'tuner.'"; the brief calls for a "strong master + sub-brand naming" structure and explicitly rules out "mascots, vintage badges, heavy gradients." This slot pairs that with the parallel Freelancer.com vinyl-sticker brief's concrete print scope: "scalable layouts for several sizes (small shelf labels up to large window pieces)", "print-ready artwork optimised for high-tack, weather-resistant vinyl", files prepped "for digital printing—complete with bleed, trim marks and cut lines", and "Final vector artwork (AI/SVG) and a press-ready PDF for each approved size." REASONABLY ENRICHED to make it a runnable Adobe-connector CREATE task: the real company/person is replaced by a fictional IP-clean brand (Apexguard Auto Film) with an invented master + two sub-brands (Apexguard SHIELD, Apexguard TINT). The client is modeled as supplying flat AI-generated raster logo concepts (one master wordmark lockup + two sub-brand marks) that carry the tell-tale AI-export defects (soft anti-aliased edges, faint off-white card, mild noise, a couple degrees of tilt) a real freelancer must clean BEFORE tracing, PLUS an authored die-cut decal-sheet vector template (decal_sheet_template.ai carrying real placement/merge frames + registered cut-lines and bleed) and a CSV of the approved sticker sizes/labels. The vector masters are produced by image_vectorize (raster->SVG, the honest connector path for "final vector artwork"); the "print-ready artwork for several sizes... with cut lines" is genuinely ASSEMBLED by binding the cleaned vector marks + the size CSV into the client's authored cut-line template via document_merge_data_vector [T], then exporting the press-ready PDF/SVG via document_render_vector [C]. AI/EPS are honestly described as export formats of the same vector master. No from-scratch layout composition and no generative capability is used; the die-line template is a supplied authored input, not connector-composed. No deliverable is invented beyond the logos + the multi-size decal/sticker sheet the briefs ask for.
one_line_ask: Clean and de-skew Apexguard's three AI-drafted PPF/tint logo rasters, trace them into crisp production SVG masters, derive one-color knockouts for vinyl weeding, then assemble a multi-size die-cut vinyl decal/sticker sheet by binding the vector marks and the approved-sizes CSV into the client's authored cut-line template and exporting a press-ready PDF + SVG.
full_brief:
Apexguard Auto Film, a fictional performance-automotive brand selling paint protection film (PPF) and window tint, is rebranding and needs its new identity turned into production-ready vector artwork and a print-ready vinyl decal sheet for its shop glass, product boxes, and installer-kit labels. The client has drafted the concepts with an AI image tool and only has flat raster PNGs: a master wordmark lockup for 'Apexguard' (a clean modern condensed sans in matte black with a single deep-blue accent chevron, premium/minimalist, no gradients), a sub-brand mark for 'Apexguard SHIELD' (the PPF line — the wordmark plus a simple hex-shield glyph), and a sub-brand mark for 'Apexguard TINT' (the window-tint line — the wordmark plus a tint-bar glyph rendered as flat bands). All three rasters carry the usual AI-export problems: soft anti-aliased edges, a faint off-white textured card behind the art, mild luminance noise, and each is tilted a couple of degrees off level. They cannot go to a vinyl cutter/printer as-is because they do not scale, have no clean cut path, and have no transparency. The client also supplies (a) an authored die-cut decal-sheet template (decal_sheet_template.ai) laid out on a single sheet with several registered sticker cells at different sizes — small installer-kit labels up to a large window/glass piece — each cell carrying a real placement/merge field plus a registered cut-line and bleed, and (b) a CSV of the approved sticker sizes and cell labels (cell_label, size_in, which_mark, mark) that matches the template's merge fields.

The job: (1) ingest the three raster logos and get clean working copies; auto-straighten each tilted mark and tonally clean it so the black is true, the blue accent is crisp, and the near-white card is even; (2) crop each mark tight to its true artwork bounds and knock the background to transparent so the trace has no halo; (3) VECTORIZE each cleaned mark into a crisp, fully-scalable SVG master preserving the exact black + deep-blue palette — these three SVGs are the primary editable/print-ready vector deliverables (AI/EPS are the same master exported to those formats); (4) from the master wordmark, derive a single-color / knockout vector (solid-fill silhouette) for one-color window vinyl, etched glass and weeding-friendly cutter output, plus a white-on-dark knockout for dark boxes and tinted glass; (5) ASSEMBLE the multi-size vinyl decal/sticker sheet the briefs ask for: upload the cleaned vector marks, then run the client's authored decal_sheet_template.ai through a vector data-merge against the approved-sizes CSV so each registered cell is populated with the correct mark at the correct size behind its cut-line and bleed, and export the assembled sheet to a press-ready PDF and a production SVG; (6) recommend a brand-safe font pairing that matches the wordmark so the client can extend the identity across packaging and web. Keep it premium, minimalist and confident — no gradients on the master, no mascots, no vintage badges — and preserve the black + deep-blue palette exactly. Sizes and cell labels stay exactly as given in the CSV.

inputs.name: apexguard_master_wordmark_raw.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A 1600x1000 flat PNG of an AI-generated automotive-brand master wordmark logo as it would look straight out of an image generator: the word 'APEXGUARD' set in a clean modern condensed sans-serif in matte near-black (#161719), with a single small deep-blue (#123A6B) accent chevron sitting just left of the 'A', and a thin all-caps tagline 'AUTO FILM - PPF & TINT' beneath. Premium, minimalist, performance-automotive feel, no gradients, no mascot, no badge. The whole lockup sits centered on a faint off-white (#F3F1EC) softly textured paper card that fills the frame. Give it the tell-tale AI-export traits: very slightly soft anti-aliased edges, faint luminance noise across the card, mildly uneven lighting, and the whole logo rotated about 2.5 degrees clockwise so the baseline is not level. Crisp readable type, accurate kerning, no watermark, no 3D gloss, flat art.
inputs.realism_notes: Must read as a believable AI export, not a pre-cleaned vector: keep the textured off-white card (no transparency), soft edges, faint noise and ~2.5deg tilt — the exact defects the chain must fix (auto_straighten, tonal cleanup, crop, background knock) before vectorizing. The single flat blue accent + true black test the 'preserve the exact palette' clause while keeping the trace clean. Flat, gradient-free art matches the brief's 'no heavy gradients' rule. Text must stay legible so the trace and font_recommend are meaningful. 8-bit sRGB PNG.

inputs.name: apexguard_shield_submark_raw.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A 1500x1100 flat PNG of an AI-generated sub-brand mark: the wordmark 'APEXGUARD' with 'SHIELD' set smaller beneath it, next to a simple flat hexagonal shield glyph outline in deep blue (#123A6B) with a matte-black core, representing a paint-protection-film product line. Minimal geometric line-art shield, no gradients, no bevel, no 3D. Centered on a faint off-white (#F1EFEA) softly textured card filling the frame. Same AI-export tells: slightly soft anti-aliased edges, faint noise, mildly uneven light, and the lockup rotated about 3 degrees COUNTER-clockwise so the baseline tips the other way. Crisp legible type, no watermark, flat art.
inputs.realism_notes: Sub-brand for the PPF line, deliberately a different tilt direction so it needs its own straighten/clean/trace pass and stays visually distinct from the master. Simple flat hex-shield glyph is ideal for a clean vectorize and for deriving a one-color knockout. 8-bit sRGB PNG.

inputs.name: apexguard_tint_submark_raw.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A 1500x1100 flat PNG of an AI-generated sub-brand mark: the wordmark 'APEXGUARD' with 'TINT' set smaller beneath it, next to a simple flat rectangular tint-bar glyph drawn as three discrete flat bands stepping from light grey to deep charcoal (NO smooth gradient — hard-edged flat bands only), representing a window-tint product line. Deep-blue (#123A6B) hairline frame around the bar, matte-black type. Minimal, no bevel, no 3D. Centered on a faint off-white (#F2F0EB) softly textured card filling the frame. AI-export tells: slightly soft edges, faint noise, mild uneven lighting, and the mark rotated about 2 degrees off-level. High-contrast flat art, crisp type, no watermark.
inputs.realism_notes: Second sub-brand for the tint line. The tint bar is authored as HARD-EDGED FLAT BANDS (not a smooth gradient) specifically so image_vectorize traces it cleanly into flat vector shapes and so it honors the brief's 'no heavy gradients' rule. Different tilt again for a distinct clean/trace pass. 8-bit sRGB PNG.

inputs.name: decal_sheet_template.ai
inputs.gen_prompt: AUTHORED DELIVERABLE (cannot be model-generated): a genuine Adobe Illustrator document (.ai) for a single-sheet die-cut vinyl decal layout. Must contain several registered sticker CELLS at different physical sizes (e.g. a large ~10in window/glass piece, a couple of ~4in box labels, and several small ~1.5in installer-kit labels), each cell carrying a REAL vector data-merge placement field (e.g. <<mark>>) plus a registered CUT-LINE path on a dedicated 'CutContour' spot layer and a bleed allowance, with trim/registration marks. Authored by hand in Illustrator so the vector data-merge actually binds the placed marks into the cells; a raster or a PDF-flattened file would NOT bind or cut.
inputs.realism_notes: Modeled as the client's own authored production template (a normal print-shop hand-off). document_merge_data_vector is [T] and REQUIRES a user-authored .ai carrying real merge fields + cut-lines — it cannot be connector-generated, so it is listed as a supplied input, not a chain output. This is what makes the task a genuine CREATE (assemble the marks into the supplied die-line sheet) rather than layout composition.

inputs.name: decal_sizes.csv
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a CSV with columns cell_label,size_in,which_mark,mark that assigns the approved sticker sizes to the template cells: one large 10in window piece using the master wordmark, two 4in product-box labels using the SHIELD and TINT sub-marks, and four small 1.5in installer-kit labels alternating the three marks. The 'mark' column holds the merge token that maps to the placed vector (master / shield / tint). Column names must exactly match the placement/merge fields in decal_sheet_template.ai so the vector data-merge binds.
inputs.realism_notes: Honors the vinyl brief's 'scalable layouts for several sizes (small shelf labels up to large window pieces)' literally; column headers match the authored .ai merge fields so the [T] vector data-merge binds each cleaned mark into the right cell at the right size.

outputs.name: apexguard_master_wordmark.svg
outputs.spec: Clean full-color vector trace of the Apexguard master wordmark lockup, matte-black type + single deep-blue accent chevron preserved exactly, transparent background, crisp scalable paths; the primary editable master (AI/EPS are this master exported to those formats).

outputs.name: apexguard_shield_submark.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vector trace of the Apexguard SHIELD sub-brand mark (wordmark + flat hex-shield glyph), palette preserved, transparent background, scalable paths; PPF-line sub-brand master.

outputs.name: apexguard_tint_submark.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vector trace of the Apexguard TINT sub-brand mark (wordmark + flat-band tint-bar glyph), flat bands traced as discrete shapes, transparent background, scalable paths; tint-line sub-brand master.

outputs.name: apexguard_master_onecolor_knockout.svg
outputs.spec: Single-color knockout/silhouette vector of the master wordmark (solid fill on flattened field) for one-color window vinyl, etched glass, weeding-friendly cutter output and single-color print.

outputs.name: apexguard_master_white_on_dark.png
outputs.spec: White-on-dark one-color knockout of the master wordmark, hi-res transparent PNG (white silhouette on transparency) for dark product boxes and tinted glass panels.

outputs.name: apexguard_decal_sheet_print.pdf
outputs.spec: Press-ready single-sheet die-cut vinyl decal PDF assembled from the authored decal_sheet_template.ai via vector data-merge: each registered cell populated with the correct cleaned vector mark at the correct approved size, cut-lines on the CutContour spot layer, bleed and trim/registration marks intact, vector/~300 DPI print quality.

outputs.name: apexguard_decal_sheet_production.svg
outputs.spec: Production SVG export of the same assembled multi-size decal sheet (marks placed in their registered cells with cut-lines) for the vinyl cutter/plotter workflow.

outputs.name: apexguard_font_pairing.json
outputs.spec: Recommended brand-safe font family + heading/body pairing matching the wordmark style, from font_recommend, so the client can extend the identity across packaging and web.

connector_workflow.note: Begin uploading the client's three supplied raster logo concepts (master + SHIELD + TINT) into Creative Cloud storage to start the pipeline.
Finalize the upload to stable CC asset URIs for the three rasters; every later step consumes a prior output. This is the single initial asset load.
Correct the ~2.5deg clockwise tilt on the Apexguard master wordmark so the baseline is level — straight art traces cleaner.
Auto-tone the straightened master to normalize the mild AI lighting and recover clean black/near-white separation for a sharp trace.
Boost only the deep-blue accent channel so the brand blue is punchy and clean-edged for the trace (black + card untouched).
Trim the surrounding card so the canvas hugs the master artwork, removing wasted background before knocking it out.

Knock the faint textured card to true transparency so the trace has no halo/fringe around the letterforms and chevron. This cleaned master is the reuse hub for the master SVG, the knockouts and the decal placement.
Vectorize the cleaned master wordmark into a crisp full-color SVG master, preserving the matte black + deep-blue chevron exactly (deliverable; AI/EPS derive from it).
Straighten the SHIELD sub-mark, tilted ~3deg the OTHER way, so its baseline is level for a clean trace.
Auto-tone the SHIELD mark so the hex-shield glyph and type separate crisply from the off-white card.
Crop the SHIELD lockup tight to its true artwork bounds.
Knock the SHIELD card to transparency for a halo-free trace and clean decal placement.
Vectorize the cleaned SHIELD sub-mark into a scalable SVG master, hex-shield glyph + palette preserved (deliverable).
Level the TINT sub-mark (~2deg off) so the flat tint-bar bands and type trace true.
Auto-tone the TINT mark to maximize contrast between the flat tint bands so they trace as clean discrete shapes.
Crop the TINT lockup tight to its bounds.
Knock the TINT card to transparency for a halo-free trace and clean decal placement.
Vectorize the cleaned TINT sub-mark into a scalable SVG master, flat tint-bar bands traced as discrete shapes (deliverable).
Flatten the isolated master wordmark to a single solid color (color overlay over the transparent cut-out) to build the one-color knockout source for window vinyl.
Vectorize the solid-color master into the one-color knockout SVG for single-color window vinyl, etched glass and weeding-friendly cutter output (deliverable).
Apply a solid white color overlay to the isolated master wordmark -> white-on-dark knockout PNG (white silhouette on transparency) for dark boxes and tinted glass (deliverable).
Upload the three cleaned vector marks so the authored decal template can reference them by URN for the placement/merge.
Finalize the upload; returns the vector asset URNs the decal_sheet_template.ai merge fields will reference.

Vector data-merge: bind each approved size/cell/mark row into the client's authored decal_sheet_template.ai (which carries real placement <<fields>> + registered cut-lines and bleed), so every cell is populated with the correct cleaned vector mark at the correct size. [T] requires the user-authored template — this is the assemble step that produces the multi-size decal sheet.
Export the merged decal-sheet .ai to a press-ready PDF with cut-lines, bleed and registration marks intact (deliverable).
Export the same merged sheet to a production SVG for the vinyl cutter/plotter workflow (deliverable).
Recommend a brand-safe font family + heading/body pairing matching the wordmark style so the client can extend the identity across packaging and web (deliverable).

chaining_note: Single initial asset load at steps 1-2 (upload the three supplied rasters -> raw_asset_uris); every subsequent step consumes a prior output. Three parallel clean->trace sub-chains seed from raw_asset_uris (the legitimate first link of each mark pipeline): master = straighten(3)->auto_tone(4)->single_color_saturation blue(5)->crop_to_bounds(6)->remove_background(7)->vectorize(8); SHIELD = straighten(9)->auto_tone(10)->crop(11)->remove_background(12)->vectorize(13); TINT = straighten(14)->auto_tone(15)->crop(16)->remove_background(17)->vectorize(18). The cleaned master (master_clean_transparent, out of 7) is a reuse hub feeding two knockout branches: color_overlay solid(19)->vectorize(20) = one-color knockout SVG, and color_overlay white(21) = white-on-dark PNG. The three vector masters (8,13,18) converge into a second upload(22)->finalize(23) so the authored template can reference them, then document_merge_data_vector(24) binds them + the decal_sizes.csv into the client's authored decal_sheet_template.ai to ASSEMBLE the multi-size sheet. That one merged .ai(24) is the single source for both exports — render PDF(25) and render SVG(26). font_recommend(27) reads the master SVG's wordmark style. Nothing is loaded fresh after step 2 except the authored template + size CSV consumed at the merge; the decal sheet emerges entirely from the chain.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon and wide-mix: 27 connector calls, 12 distinct Adobe tools across four groups (asset ingest/storage, the image_* raster/vector family, the document_* vector-merge/render family, and font recommendation) — far above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Ordering matters — each mark must be straightened, tonally cleaned, cropped and background-knocked BEFORE vectorizing or the trace inherits the AI noise/tilt/halo and fails the 'clean, fully-scalable, print-ready vector' bar; a naive agent that vectorizes the raw PNGs fails. It runs three separate marks through their own consistent clean->trace pipelines (a master plus two sub-brands with different tilts), then reuses the cleaned master as a hub to spawn the one-color knockout (color_overlay -> re-vectorize) and the white-on-dark knockout. The genuinely hard convergence is the CREATE step: the three vector masters plus the approved-sizes CSV are bound into the client's AUTHORED die-cut template via document_merge_data_vector [T] to assemble a real multi-size decal sheet with cut-lines and bleed, then dual-exported (press PDF + production SVG) via document_render_vector [C]. It respects every hard limit: no generative fill / object-removal / text-to-image / reframe-crop / upscaling / from-scratch layout; image_fill_area is not used; there is no generative capability; the vector masters come solely from image_vectorize (raster->SVG) and AI/EPS are honestly export formats of that master; the decal sheet is ASSEMBLED into a supplied authored .ai template (a supplied input), and document_render_vector only EXPORTS that genuine authored/merged vector rather than composing a layout. The branch-and-converge topology (three cleaned marks fanning into knockouts, then reconverging through a real vector data-merge into an assembled multi-size sheet and dual export) exercises multi-output reuse and a genuine authored-template assemble end to end.

reverify.banned_capability_check: CONFIRMED none of the banned capabilities are used: no image_crop_and_resize / reframe-crop / reframe-crop; no generative fill of new content; no text-to-image or from-scratch art/logo (the three logo concepts and the die-cut template are SUPPLIED inputs, the vector masters come only from image_vectorize tracing the supplied rasters, and the decal sheet is assembled by binding those traced marks into the client's AUTHORED .ai template — not authored from scratch by the connector); no upscaling / super-resolution; no object-removal-with-reconstruction / inpainting of occluded pixels; no bespoke font/letterform design (font_recommend only RECOMMENDS an existing family); no unreachable external account. document_merge_data_vector is correctly tagged [T] and the authored decal_sheet_template.ai with real merge fields + cut-lines is a supplied input (kind:data, gen_model:'n/a-authored-asset'); CSV headers match the merge fields; document_render_vector only EXPORTS the genuine merged authored vector, so there is no false 'authored vector composed by connector' claim. All 27 steps are server=='adobe'.
reverify.notes: Reverified against realism, spec-parity, feasibility and slot-match; severity=pass. REALISM: grounds in the real Upwork 'Logo and Brand Identity Designer' (United Auto Film PPF/tint rebrand) + the Freelancer.com vinyl-sticker brief; grounding_note quotes verbatim ('paint protection film (PPF), window tint...', 'Premium, modern, performance-automotive. Minimalist and confident...', 'mascots, vintage badges, heavy gradients', 'scalable layouts for several sizes (small shelf labels up to large window pieces)', 'Final vector artwork (AI/SVG) and a press-ready PDF for each approved size'); fictional IP-clean brand Apexguard Auto Film with invented master + two sub-brands. SPEC-PARITY: every field present and as rich as the AO-14 example; inputs carry realistic AI-export defects (soft edges, off-white card, noise, tilt) + an authored .ai template + a merge-matched CSV; outputs enumerate the three SVG masters, two knockouts, dual decal-sheet exports, and font pairing; counts consistent (27 workflow steps = tool_call_count=27; 12 distinct adobe tools = distinct_adobe_tools=12; tools_used set == workflow tool set). FEASIBILITY: each potentially-risky step stays in-bounds — image_vectorize (n8,n13,n18,n20) is the sanctioned raster->SVG trace, the honest path for 'final vector artwork (AI/SVG)'; AI/EPS are described as export formats of that master, not separately authored. image_remove_background (n7,n12,n17) outputs transparent PNG only, used to de-halo before tracing, not compositing. image_apply_color_overlay (n19 solid, n21 white) recolors the isolated master to a single solid color for the one-color and white-on-dark knockouts — a flatten/recolor, not generative. image_adjust_single_color_saturation (n5) crisps only the blue channel. image_auto_straighten / image_apply_auto_tone / image_crop_to_bounds are standard headless raster edits. document_merge_data_vector (n24) [T] only BINDS supplied vector marks + CSV into the supplied authored template; document_render_vector (n25,n26) [C] only EXPORTS the merged genuine vector — neither composes a layout from scratch. image_fill_area is NOT used, so no solid-fill-area edge cases. font_recommend and asset_* (accepted by AO-14/AO-22/AO-19 precedent) only search/store/present. SLOT-MATCH: category=vectorize_logo, vertical=automotive_transport, workflow_nature=create (a NEW deliverable — the assembled multi-size die-cut decal/sticker sheet — is assembled from supplied parts: the traced vector marks + the authored cut-line template + the sizes CSV, with no generative step), output_modality=vector (three SVG masters + a production-SVG sheet), operation/family = vectorize/vector-print. inputs_from is emitted as comma-joined strings per the StructuredOutput schema (schema types inputs_from as string, unlike the file-form arrays). No rewrites of out-of-bounds steps were needed; the authored chain was already clean.
